risks: NSG SSH rule allows inbound from all sources (sourceAddressPrefix: *), exposing port 22 to the public internet
VM uses password authentication (disablePasswordAuthentication: false) instead of SSH key-based auth
Cosmos DB has automatic failover disabled (enableAutomaticFailover: false) in a single-region deployment, risking write unavailability during outages
All public-facing services (IoT Hub, Cosmos DB, Cognitive Services, Key Vault) have publicNetworkAccess set to Enabled without private endpoints
Key Vault purge protection is disabled (enablePurgeProtection: false), risking permanent and irrecoverable secret deletion
No monitoring or diagnostics resources (Log Analytics workspace, Application Insights) included for operational visibility
Single-region deployment with no geo-redundancy or availability zone configuration for any resource
No managed identity resources defined for secure service-to-service authentication between VM, Logic App, IoT Hub, and Cosmos DB
correctionsRecommended: Restrict NSG SSH rule sourceAddressPrefix to specific trusted IP ranges or a bastion subnet instead of wildcard *
Set VM osProfile.linuxConfiguration.disablePasswordAuthentication to true and provision SSH public keys
Enable Cosmos DB automatic failover (enableAutomaticFailover: true) and consider adding a secondary read region
Enable Key Vault purge protection (enablePurgeProtection: true) per Azure best practices
Add Log Analytics workspace and Application Insights resources for centralized monitoring and diagnostics
Add private endpoints for IoT Hub, Cosmos DB, Key Vault, and Cognitive Services to restrict network access
Add user-assigned or system-assigned managed identity for the VM and Logic App to eliminate credential-based access
Add a Network Interface (NIC) resource explicitly for the VM to clarify subnet and NSG association
Consider adding a second Cosmos DB region and enabling availability zones for production reliability
